# Story 1.7: Store Discovered Schema in MongoDB

Status: done

<!-- Generated by BMAD Ultimate Story Context Engine -->
<!-- Story File: 1-7-store-discovered-schema-in-mongodb.md -->
<!-- Epic: 1 - Automatic Schema Discovery -->
<!-- Created: 2026-03-14 -->

## Story

As a SearchAI administrator,
I want discovered schemas persisted in MongoDB,
So that field mappings can reference discovered field metadata.

## Acceptance Criteria

**Given** SchemaDiscoveryService successfully discovers a connector schema
**When** schema is persisted to MongoDB
**Then** system creates/updates DiscoveredSchema document with structure: `{tenantId, connectorId, knowledgeBaseId, fields: [{name, type, path, enumCandidates?, metadata}], discoveredAt, discoveryMethod: 'api'|'hybrid'}`
**And** fields array includes all discovered fields with detected types
**And** enumCandidates array populated for fields identified as enum candidates (field names + cardinality)
**And** MongoDB compound index on `{tenantId, knowledgeBaseId, connectorId}` ensures efficient queries per NFR-SC6
**And** discovery timestamp (discoveredAt) tracked for staleness detection
**And** upsert operation used (update existing or insert new) to handle re-discovery
**And** all write operations scoped to tenantId per project patterns
**And** tenant isolation is enforced (tenantId scoped in all operations per NFR-S1)
**And** structured logging via createLogger includes context (tenantId, connectorId, knowledgeBaseId)

## Epic Context

**Epic 1 Goal:** Admins can automatically discover field schemas from connectors with detected types and enum candidates

**Epic 1 Stories (for cross-story context):**

- Story 1.1 (DONE): Schema Discovery Service Foundation -- abstract interface created
- Story 1.2 (DONE): SharePoint API Schema Discovery -- API-described types, Choice field enums
- Story 1.3 (DONE): Google Sheets API Schema Discovery -- hybrid discovery with data sampling, `detectEnumCandidates`
- Story 1.4 (DONE): JSON Document Schema Inference -- recursive traversal with dot-notation paths
- Story 1.5 (DONE): CSV File Schema Parsing -- flat columns, expanded boolean/date, enum threshold 30
- Story 1.6 (DONE): Connector-Type Template Enum Patterns -- static enum patterns + enrichment service
- **Story 1.7** (THIS STORY): Store Discovered Schema in MongoDB
- Story 1.8: Schema Discovery Worker (BullMQ)
- Story 1.9: Schema Discovery API Endpoint

**This Story's Role:** Stories 1.1-1.6 built the discovery engine (in-memory). This story bridges that engine to persistent storage. The BullMQ worker (Story 1.8) and API endpoint (Story 1.9) depend on this persistence layer. Epic 2 (Field Mapping) reads DiscoveredSchema documents from MongoDB to generate mapping suggestions.

**Key Differences from Previous Stories:**

| Aspect     | Stories 1.1-1.6                        | Story 1.7                                           |
| ---------- | -------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| Package    | `packages/search-ai-internal` only     | `packages/database` + `packages/search-ai-internal` |
| Output     | In-memory `DiscoveredSchema` interface | MongoDB document with indexes and upsert            |
| Dependency | No external deps (pure functions)      | Mongoose, `@agent-platform/database`                |
| Testing    | Unit tests with mock data              | Unit tests + may need vitest mocking of Mongoose    |

### Relationship to Existing ConnectorSchema Model

**CRITICAL -- DO NOT CONFUSE THESE:**

The codebase already has `ConnectorSchema` in `packages/database/src/models/connector-schema.model.ts`. This is a **different model** for a different purpose:

| Aspect              | ConnectorSchema (existing)                                                     | DiscoveredSchema (this story)                                                                             |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Purpose             | Layer 1 -- raw connector fields from sync                                      | Enriched discovery with type inference + enums                                                            |
| Created by          | Connector sync workers                                                         | SchemaDiscoveryService pipeline (Stories 1.1-1.6)                                                         |
| Has knowledgeBaseId | No                                                                             | Yes -- links to SearchIndex.\_id                                                                          |
| Field metadata      | `IConnectorSchemaField`: path, label, type, isCustom, isRequired, sampleValues | `IDiscoveredSchemaField`: name, type, path, enumValues, enumDisplayNames, enumSource, description, format |
| Enum source         | Raw API values only                                                            | Template + inferred with source tracking                                                                  |
| discoveryMethod     | Not tracked                                                                    | 'api' or 'hybrid'                                                                                         |

### DiscoveredSchema Interface (Service Layer -- Source of Truth)

This is what the discovery services return (from `SchemaDiscoveryService.ts`):

```typescript
export interface DiscoveredField {
  name: string;
  type: string; // 'string' | 'number' | 'date' | 'boolean' | 'array'
  path: string;
  metadata: {
    description?: string;
    required?: boolean;
    enumValues?: string[];
    format?: string;
    enumDisplayNames?: Record<string, string>; // Added Story 1.6
    enumSource?: 'template' | 'inferred'; // Added Story 1.6
  };
}

export interface DiscoveredSchema {
  connectorId: string;
  tenantId: string;
  fields: DiscoveredField[];
  discoveryMethod: 'api' | 'hybrid';
  discoveredAt: Date;
  metadata: {
    connectorType: string;
    version?: string;
  };
}
```

**What the MongoDB model must ADD beyond this interface:**

- `_id` (UUIDv7)
- `knowledgeBaseId` (links to SearchIndex.\_id -- passed in by caller, not part of discovery)
- `version` (for re-discovery versioning)
- `fieldCount` (computed from fields.length for quick queries)
- `status` ('draft' | 'active')
- `_v` (optimistic concurrency)
- `createdAt`, `updatedAt` (timestamps)

### MongoDB Model Design

**File:** `packages/database/src/models/discovered-schema.model.ts`

**Follow the exact pattern from `connector-schema.model.ts`:**

```typescript
// IDiscoveredSchemaField -- maps to DiscoveredField from service layer
export interface IDiscoveredSchemaField {
  name: string;
  type: string;
  path: string;
  description?: string;
  required?: boolean;
  enumValues?: string[];
  format?: string;
  enumDisplayNames?: Record<string, string>;
  enumSource?: 'template' | 'inferred';
}

// IDiscoveredSchema -- the MongoDB document
export interface IDiscoveredSchema {
  _id: string;
  tenantId: string;
  connectorId: string;
  knowledgeBaseId: string; // Links to SearchIndex._id
  version: number;
  fields: IDiscoveredSchemaField[];
  fieldCount: number;
  discoveryMethod: 'api' | 'hybrid';
  discoveredAt: Date;
  status: string; // 'draft' | 'active'
  metadata: {
    connectorType: string;
    version?: string;
  };
  _v: number;
  createdAt: Date;
  updatedAt: Date;
}
```

**Indexes (from AC):**

- `{ tenantId: 1, knowledgeBaseId: 1, connectorId: 1 }` -- compound unique (for upsert)
- `{ tenantId: 1, connectorId: 1 }` -- connector-scoped queries
- `{ tenantId: 1, discoveredAt: -1 }` -- staleness queries

**Plugins:** `tenantIsolationPlugin`

### Persistence Service Design

**File:** `packages/search-ai-internal/src/services/DiscoveredSchemaPersistenceService.ts`

This service converts the service-layer `DiscoveredSchema` to the MongoDB document and handles upsert logic.

```typescript
export interface PersistDiscoveredSchemaOptions {
  schema: DiscoveredSchema; // From discovery pipeline
  knowledgeBaseId: string; // Links to SearchIndex._id
}

export interface DiscoveredSchemaPersistenceService {
  /**
   * Upsert a discovered schema to MongoDB.
   * Uses compound key {tenantId, knowledgeBaseId, connectorId} for upsert.
   * Increments version on update, sets version=1 on insert.
   */
  upsertDiscoveredSchema(options: PersistDiscoveredSchemaOptions): Promise<IDiscoveredSchema>;

  /**
   * Get the latest discovered schema for a connector.
   * Returns null if no schema has been discovered yet.
   */
  getDiscoveredSchema(
    tenantId: string,
    knowledgeBaseId: string,
    connectorId: string,
  ): Promise<IDiscoveredSchema | null>;

  /**
   * Get all discovered schemas for a knowledge base.
   * Used by FieldsTab UI to show all connector schemas.
   */
  getSchemasByKnowledgeBase(
    tenantId: string,
    knowledgeBaseId: string,
  ): Promise<IDiscoveredSchema[]>;
}
```

**Upsert Logic:**

```typescript
// findOneAndUpdate with upsert:true
const result = await DiscoveredSchemaModel.findOneAndUpdate(
  { tenantId, knowledgeBaseId, connectorId }, // filter
  {
    $set: { fields, fieldCount, discoveryMethod, discoveredAt, status: 'active', metadata },
    $inc: { version: 1, _v: 1 },
    $setOnInsert: { _id: uuidv7() },
  },
  { upsert: true, new: true },
);
```

### Established Patterns from Stories 1.1-1.6 (CRITICAL -- Follow These)

1. **Logger name must be unique** -- Use `'discovered-schema-persistence'`
2. **ESM imports with .js extension** -- All relative imports
3. **No new npm dependencies** -- Uses existing Mongoose from `@agent-platform/database`
4. **Error pattern** -- `err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)`
5. **TODO(Story 1.8) comments** -- At persist start/complete/error for TraceEvent emission
6. **Import from barrel** -- `import { DiscoveredSchema, type IDiscoveredSchema } from '@agent-platform/database/models'`
7. **Build before test** -- `pnpm build` then `pnpm test`
8. **Prettier before commit** -- `npx prettier --write <files>`

### Model Pattern Reference (from connector-schema.model.ts)

```typescript
import mongoose, { Schema, model } from 'mongoose';
import { uuidv7 } from '../mongo/base-document.js';
import { tenantIsolationPlugin } from '../mongo/plugins/tenant-isolation.plugin.js';

// Sub-document schema: { _id: false }
const FieldSchema = new Schema<IField>({ ... }, { _id: false });

// Document schema: timestamps + collection name
const DocSchema = new Schema<IDoc>(
  { _id: { type: String, default: uuidv7 }, ... },
  { timestamps: true, collection: 'discovered_schemas' },
);

DocSchema.plugin(tenantIsolationPlugin);
DocSchema.index({ ... }, { unique: true });

export const Model = (mongoose.models.Model as any) || model<IDoc>('Model', DocSchema);
```

### Testing Requirements

**Test File:** `packages/search-ai-internal/src/services/__tests__/DiscoveredSchemaPersistenceService.test.ts`

**Challenge:** This service depends on Mongoose/MongoDB. Two approaches:

1. **Mock Mongoose model** -- Use `vi.mock` to mock the database model import. This keeps tests fast and avoids MongoMemoryServer (which is excluded from fast CI tier per MEMORY.md).
2. **Test the conversion logic separately** -- Extract the service-to-model conversion as a pure function, test that directly.

**Recommended approach: Mock the Mongoose model.** The persistence service is thin -- the critical logic is the upsert filter, the field conversion, and the version increment.

**Required Test Cases:**

1. **Upsert -- insert new schema:**
   - Given no existing schema for {tenantId, knowledgeBaseId, connectorId}
   - When upsertDiscoveredSchema called
   - Then creates new document with version=1, status='active'
   - And fieldCount matches fields.length
   - And all field metadata preserved (enumValues, enumDisplayNames, enumSource)

2. **Upsert -- update existing schema:**
   - Given existing schema for {tenantId, knowledgeBaseId, connectorId}
   - When upsertDiscoveredSchema called with new fields
   - Then updates document, increments version
   - And discoveredAt updated to new timestamp

3. **Get schema -- found:**
   - Returns IDiscoveredSchema with all fields when schema exists

4. **Get schema -- not found:**
   - Returns null when no schema exists for the filter

5. **Get schemas by knowledge base:**
   - Returns array of schemas for the given tenantId + knowledgeBaseId

6. **Field conversion:**
   - Service-layer DiscoveredField.metadata flattened correctly to IDiscoveredSchemaField
   - enumDisplayNames preserved as Record<string, string>
   - enumSource preserved

7. **Tenant isolation:**
   - All queries include tenantId in filter

8. **Error handling:**
   - Mongoose errors propagated with structured logging
   - Logger called with context (tenantId, connectorId, knowledgeBaseId)

**Mock Strategy:**

```typescript
// Mock the database model
vi.mock('@agent-platform/database/models', () => ({
  DiscoveredSchema: {
    findOneAndUpdate: vi.fn(),
    findOne: vi.fn(),
    find: vi.fn(),
  },
}));
```

### Previous Story Intelligence (Stories 1.1-1.6)

**Learnings from Story 1.6 code review (most recent):**

1. **Pattern match via fieldPatterns** -- Strategy 2 in resolveEnumPattern was broken because it didn't pass the full template. Fixed in code review. Lesson: always verify function receives the data it needs.
2. **Early return consistency** -- The empty-enumPatterns path didn't tag inferred fields. Fixed in review. Lesson: ensure all code paths produce consistent output.
3. **Unused imports** -- `type ConnectorTypeTemplate` was imported but never used in test. Lesson: clean up imports.

**Learnings from Story 1.5 code review:**

1. **Duplicate handling** -- indexOf bug silently mapped duplicate headers to first column's data. Lesson: test edge cases with duplicate inputs.
2. **TODO at EVERY error path** -- Don't miss error paths for TraceEvent TODOs.

**Pattern established across ALL stories:**

- Every service has unique logger name
- Every service file goes in `packages/search-ai-internal/src/services/`
- Every service is exported via barrel `index.ts`
- Tests use `describe/test/expect/vi` from vitest
- Tests use factory functions for mock data (`makeDiscoveredSchema(...)`)
- Immutable patterns preferred (return new objects, don't mutate)

### What This Story Does NOT Include

**Out of Scope (deferred to later stories):**

- BullMQ worker calling persistence -- Story 1.8
- API endpoint exposing discovered schemas -- Story 1.9
- Field mapping generation from discovered schemas -- Epic 2
- FieldsTab UI reading discovered schemas -- Epic 3
- TraceEvent emission (TODO comments only) -- Story 1.8
- Schema versioning UI (showing diffs between versions) -- Future epic
- Schema deletion/cleanup cron -- Future epic

**This Story Focus:**

- Create `IDiscoveredSchema` and `IDiscoveredSchemaField` interfaces in database package
- Create Mongoose model with compound index and tenantIsolationPlugin
- Export model from database barrel and package.json
- Create persistence service with upsert, get, and list methods
- Create unit tests with mocked Mongoose model
- Ensure field metadata (enumValues, enumDisplayNames, enumSource) roundtrips correctly

## Dev Agent Record

### Agent Model Used

Claude Opus 4.6 (us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-6-v1)

### Debug Log References

No issues encountered. All builds and tests passed on first attempt.

### Completion Notes List

- All 5 tasks completed with 15 tests, all passing.
- Created `discovered-schema.model.ts` in database package following exact `connector-schema.model.ts` pattern.
- `IDiscoveredSchemaField` flattens the service-layer `DiscoveredField.metadata` into top-level fields for clean MongoDB storage.
- Compound unique index on `{tenantId, knowledgeBaseId, connectorId}` enables upsert pattern.
- Persistence service uses dependency injection for the Mongoose model (passed as parameter) — enables clean mocking without `vi.mock` of external modules.
- `toSchemaField` exported as a pure conversion function for independent testing.
- All read queries use `.lean()` for performance.
- TODO(Story 1.8) comments at persist start/complete/error paths.
- Note: `$setOnInsert: { _id: uuidv7() }` from story spec was omitted — Mongoose `default: uuidv7` on the schema handles this automatically on insert.
